channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WGN 9

item.title: Detained American missionary released in Tunisia after 13 months
item.description: A Trump administration official said that an American missionary was released Sunday after 13 months of detainment in Tunisia, Reuters reported. Adam Boehler, a U.S. special envoy, said that Robert Vieira’s release was secured Sunday, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hamas says it's sending a delegation to Qatar to continue Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hamas official said Monday that the Palestinian militant group is sending a delegation to the Gulf state of Qatar to continue indirect ceasefire talks with Israel over the war in Gaza, as the territory’s Health Ministry said that 38 people were confirmed dead over
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump blames Zelensky for Ukraine war after '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: President Trump on Monday sought to distance himself from the war between Russia and Ukraine as his promise to end the conflict quickly has proven difficult to accomplish. "The War between Russia and Ukraine is Biden’s war, not mine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland says Moscow is 'mocking' Trump with deadly Ukraine strike
item.description: Poland is urging the U.S. to respond to Russian President Vladimir Putin's "mocking" response to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure peace deal by striking civilians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers push to meet with Salvadoran president to plead case of mistakenly deported man
item.description: Lawmakers are pushing to meet with Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele while he’s in Washington amid broader efforts to secure the return of a Maryland man wrongfully deported to a prison in the Central American nation. Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) sent a letter to El Salvador’s ambassador to the U.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Hungary passes constitutional amendment to ban LGBTQ+ public events, seen as a major blow to rights
item.description: Hungary’s parliament has passed an amendment to the constitution that allows the government to ban public events by LGBTQ+ communities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: Social media company Meta says that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after privacy activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: College student released in Denmark after alleged Uber dispute
item.description: Copenhagen police said the pair has been charged with common assault and is expected to stay in detention until April 24.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Former Malaysian leader Abdullah Ahmad Badawi, who extended political freedoms, dies at 85
item.description: Former Malaysian Prime Minister Abdullah Ahmad Badawi, a moderate who extended the country’s political freedoms but was criticized for lackluster leadership, has died of heart disease.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes kill at least 7 people in Yemen, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes around Yemen's rebel-held capital killed at least seven people and wounded 29 overnight, the Houthis said Monday as they also claimed shooting down another American MQ-9 Reaper drone.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump criticizes Iran for 'tapping us along' with second round of nuclear talks set for Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump is complaining about the pace of nuclear negotiations between the United States and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Maria Grazia Murru And Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump trade war with China hurting US farmers, advocate says
item.description: John Boyd Jr., a fourth-generation family farmer who works 1,300 acres in Virginia, said he hopes the Trump administration will listen to farmers moving forward.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Home to centuries of heritage, Sudan's biggest museum is looted and wrecked by a 2-year war
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Inside Sudan's biggest museum, the exhibition halls once filled with statues and relics from centuries of ancient civilizations are trashed, littered with debris. The display cases stand empty and shattered. A mummy lies exposed in an open storage box.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kellogg condemns Russia's attack on Palm Sunday gathering in Ukraine
item.description: Keith Kellogg, President Trump’s special envoy to Ukraine, condemned Russia’s attack on a Palm Sunday gathering in Ukraine. “Today's Palm Sunday attack by Russian forces on civilian targets in Sumy crosses any line of decency,” Kellogg said in a post on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump administration's foreign aid cuts threaten to roll back global maternal mortality progress
item.description: Maternal mortality rates have declined significantly across the world since the turn of the century, but global health experts fear that progress could be rolled back as a result of the Trump administration's cuts to U.S. foreign aid.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, The Hill

item.title: Israel intensifies strikes across Gaza on Palm Sunday and hits a hospital in the north
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza (AP) — A wave of Israeli strikes across Gaza on Sunday hit a hospital, a municipal building, a home and a vehicle, killing at least 21 people, including children, as Israel vowed to expand its security presence in the small coastal strip.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Convalescing Pope Francis opens Holy Week with in-person greeting in St. Peter's Square
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A convalescing Pope Francis greeted the crowd in St. Peter’s Square on Palm Sunday, wishing more than 20,000 faithful a “Happy Palm Sunday, Happy Holy Week," in yet another reassuring public sign of his recovery from a life-threatening battle with double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fears of another narco-state rise as Latin American country readies for pivotal vote
item.description: Ecuador is headed to a second round of a presidential election in which incumbent President Daniel Noboa faces leftist challenger Luisa González as the nation faces gang violence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Russia-Ukraine talks at 'put up or shut up' point
item.description: Trump's comments came after a top envoy traveled to Russia to meet directly with President Vladimir Putin on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza's southern city of Rafah and vows to 'vigorously' expand in the territory
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel announced Saturday it had completed construction of a new security corridor cutting off the southern city of Rafah from the rest of Gaza, and the military said it would soon expand “vigorously” in most of the small coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dominican club collapse death toll rises to 226 after woman pulled from rubble dies in hospital
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — A woman hospitalized after being rescued from the rubble of a roof collapse at a popular nightclub in the Dominican Republic died on Saturday, raising the death toll to 226, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu slams Carney's reply to anti-Israel agitator who said there's a 'genocide' in Gaza
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is demanding Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney "backtrack" his comments after the Canadian leader appeared to agree with a protester who said there's a "genocide" in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Serbian capital braces for a mega pro-president rally as tensions surge in the Balkan country
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Chanting patriotic songs, thousands of supporters of Serbia’s populist President Aleksandar Vucic gathered in downtown Belgrade on Friday, a day ahead of what authorities expect to be a huge rally in his support.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major nations agree on first-ever global fee on greenhouse gases with plan that targets shipping
item.description: Many of the world’s largest shipping nations decided on Friday to impose a minimum fee of $100 for every ton of greenhouse gases emitted by ships above certain thresholds, in what is effectively the first global tax on greenhouse gas emissions.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European countries vow billions in military support for Ukraine as US envoy meets Putin
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European countries vowed Friday to sends billions of dollars in further funding to help Ukraine keep fighting Russia’s invasion, as a U.S.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China ramps up military ‘rehearsals’ around Taiwan, outstrips US in air, maritime, space
item.description: Top Indo-Pacific commander warned senators that China's aggression around Taiwan is not an "exercise" but a "rehearsal" for when it finally decides to "reunify" the island with the mainland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strike on Gaza apartment building kills at least 23, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli aircraft struck a residential block in war-ravaged northern Gaza on Wednesday, killing at least 23 people, health officials said, as the renewed fighting in the devastated Palestinian enclave showed no signs of slowing.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Senate approves Peter Hoekstra as next US ambassador to Canada
item.description: The Senate approved Peter Hoekstra to be the next U.S. ambassador to Canada amid one of the toughest times in Washington's relationship with Ottawa as the tariff war heats up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: An Israeli strike hit near a charity kitchen in Gaza as Palestinians gathered for food
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on Monday hit next to a charity kitchen where Palestinians crowded to receive cooked meals as food supplies dwindle under Israel's monthlong blockade of the Gaza Strip, one of a string of attacks in the territory that killed more than 30 people, mos
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US admiral at NATO fired in expanding national security purge
item.description: U.S. Navy Vice Admiral Shoshana Chatfield is one of only a handful of female Navy three-star officers.
item.dc:creator: Phil Stewart, Idrees Ali, Lili Bayer / Reuters, NewsNation
